records.name: df45081a-be3e-4832-b36b-09edf957754b
records.notes:
Evaluation reports
The department periodically conducts evaluations of Global Affairs Canada priorities, programs and projects. Evaluation serves as a practical management tool for reviewing performance of programs and activities. The information gathered through an evaluation helps improve the design as well as the implementation of upcoming programs and initiatives.

A report is generated for each evaluation conducted.

notes_translated.en:
Evaluation reports
The department periodically conducts evaluations of Global Affairs Canada priorities, programs and projects. Evaluation serves as a practical management tool for reviewing performance of programs and activities. The information gathered through an evaluation helps improve the design as well as the implementation of upcoming programs and initiatives.

A report is generated for each evaluation conducted.
notes_translated.fr:
Rapports d'évaluation
Le Ministère des Affaires mondiales effectue périodiquement une évaluation de ses priorités, programmes et projets. L’évaluation est un outil de gestion pratique pour l’examen du rendement des programmes et des activités. Les renseignements recueillis à l’aide d’une évaluation contribuent à améliorer la conception et la mise en œuvre des programmes et des initiatives à venir.

Un rapport est produit pour chaque évaluation ayant été réalisée.

records.name: 5207c3c7-4fff-41f0-aef6-8fefdbde8000
records.notes: The Essential Skills Playbook projects map is developed to highlight projects featured as part of the “Essential Skills Playbook” published by the Office of Literacy and Essential Skills program (OLES) at Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC). The playbook is developed as a showcase for sharing promising practices, case studies and partnerships, based on OLES projects that were funded through grants and contributions generally dating back to 2012. This map allows users to visualize OLES-funded projects and explore various data variables such as the targeted groups, essential skills, and industry sectors of each project.

notes_translated.fr: La carte qui accompagne le « Guide sur les compétences essentielles » a été élaborée pour mettre en évidence les projets présentés dans le cadre du « Guide sur les compétences essentielles » publié par le Bureau de l'alphabétisation et du programme des compétences essentielles (BACE) d'Emploi et Développement Social Canada (EDSC). Le guide est conçu comme une vitrine pour le partage de pratiques prometteuses, d'études de cas et de partenariats fondés sur des projets du BACE qui ont été financés par des subventions et des contributions remontant généralement à 2012. Cette carte permet aux utilisateurs de visualiser les projets financés par le BACE et d'explorer diverses variables de données comme les groupes ciblés, les compétences essentielles et les projets de chaque secteurs industriels.
notes_translated.en: The Essential Skills Playbook projects map is developed to highlight projects featured as part of the “Essential Skills Playbook” published by the Office of Literacy and Essential Skills program (OLES) at Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC). The playbook is developed as a showcase for sharing promising practices, case studies and partnerships, based on OLES projects that were funded through grants and contributions generally dating back to 2012. This map allows users to visualize OLES-funded projects and explore various data variables such as the targeted groups, essential skills, and industry sectors of each project.

records.name: 1258aa2b-6f80-4fc3-b1f6-640742275747
records.notes: An inventory of essential skills tools and resources available across Canada to support people to improve their skills for careers in the trades.  The tools and resources can be used by trade instructors and curriculum developers to integrate essential skills into their training and other support services.

notes_translated.fr: Un inventaire ou une liste d'outils et ressources en compétences essentielles à travers le Canada sont disponibles pour appuyer les individus à améliorer leurs compétences dans un métier.  Les outils et ressources peuvent être utilisés par des instructeurs de métier et des concepteurs de programmes d'études afin d'intégrer les compétence essentielles dans la formation et tous autres services de soutien.
notes_translated.en: An inventory of essential skills tools and resources available across Canada to support people to improve their skills for careers in the trades.  The tools and resources can be used by trade instructors and curriculum developers to integrate essential skills into their training and other support services.

records.name: 16351a00-3f5c-43c0-b6a4-1a0b066abb5f
records.notes: Percentage of enterprises that took measures to overcome skill shortages, by North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code and enterprise size, based on a one-year observation period. Measures include outsourcing work in Canada, offshoring of labour, targeted recruitment process, student hiring, training staff, retention strategy, acquisition of external businesses, global talent attraction, other government programs, and other measures.

notes_translated.fr: Pourcentage d'entreprises ayant pris des mesures pour pallier les pénuries de compétences, selon le code du Système de classification des industries de l'Amérique du Nord (SCIAN) et la taille de l'entreprise, basé sur une période d'observation d'un an. Les mesures comprennent : externalisation du travail au Canada, utilisation d’une main-d’œuvre délocalisée, processus de recrutement ciblé, embauche d’étudiants, formation du personnel, stratégie de rétention, acquisition d’entreprises externes, attraction de talents à l’échelle mondiale, autres programmes gouvernementaux et autres mesures.
notes_translated.en: Percentage of enterprises that took measures to overcome skill shortages, by North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code and enterprise size, based on a one-year observation period. Measures include outsourcing work in Canada, offshoring of labour, targeted recruitment process, student hiring, training staff, retention strategy, acquisition of external businesses, global talent attraction, other government programs, and other measures.

records.name: 056f2de3-8ed7-482c-badb-c50f2c5c81eb
records.notes:
In accordance with the GoC’s Communications Policy, departments and agencies of the GoC are required to conduct a post-campaign evaluation of all major advertising initiatives exceeding $1,000,000 in media buys. In this case, post-campaign testing was conducted using the Advertising Campaign Evaluation Tool (ACET). The ACET survey instrument is a series of standardized questions to be included at the beginning of a post-campaign survey. The ACET telephone questionnaire including some additional campaign related questions took about 7-8 minutes on average to complete. 

As per above, the ACET is based on a survey of the general population and it collects information related to recall of the advertisement; recall of the key messages; who sponsored the ad; determine what, if any action has been taken as a result of seeing the ad; government performance in providing information to the public and overall government performance.

For this evaluation, NRG conducted a general public survey of 2000 Canadians 18 years of age or older.

notes_translated.fr:
À l’appui du plan en trois volets du Plan d’action économique de 2013 visant à pallier les difficultés de jumeler les Canadiens et les emplois disponibles, le gouvernement du Canada a lancé, durant la semaine du 13 mai 2013, une campagne de publicité à la télévision et dans Internet en lien avec l’initiative de formation axée sur les compétences.

La campagne sur l’initiative de formation axée sur les compétences constitue un prolongement de la campagne publicitaire Meilleurs emplois, tenue au cours des années précédentes.

Le but de la campagne publicitaire liée à l’initiative de formation axée sur les compétences consiste à sensibiliser la population canadienne en âge de travailler aux programmes et services nouveaux et actuels du gouvernement du Canada conçus pour les aider à acquérir les compétences et la formation nécessaires en vue d’obtenir, maintenant et à l’avenir, des emplois de grande qualité, bien rémunérés et où la demande est forte.

La campagne publicitaire relative à l’initiative de formation axée sur les compétences et qui a eu lieu au printemps a porté sur la sensibilisation à la Subvention canadienne pour l’emploi, une nouvelle mesure d’aide. La publicité est conçue afin d’aiguiller les adultes âgés de 18 à 50 ans vers le site Web plandaction.gc.ca/compétences ou d’inviter ces derniers à composer le 1 800 O Canada pour obtenir de plus amples renseignements. La campagne repose sur une combinaison de médias (la télévision et Internet). La publicité à la télévision, qui combine des séquences nouvelles et actuelles du Plan d’action économique, a été diffusée durant les séries éliminatoires de la Ligne nationale de hockey et sur les chaînes spécialisées. La campagne dans Internet comportait des bannières et publicités électroniques dans des sites Web grand public, du marketing dans les moteurs de recherche, ainsi que des journées de diffusion continue dans les sites rds.ca, tsn.ca, tva.canoe.ca, muchmusic.com, musiqueplus.ca, mtv.ca et youtube.com.

La campagne de publicité a été lancée la semaine du 13 mai pour une durée de sept semaines. Elle a pris fin le 30 juin 2013.
notes_translated.en:
In accordance with the GoC’s Communications Policy, departments and agencies of the GoC are required to conduct a post-campaign evaluation of all major advertising initiatives exceeding $1,000,000 in media buys. In this case, post-campaign testing was conducted using the Advertising Campaign Evaluation Tool (ACET). The ACET survey instrument is a series of standardized questions to be included at the beginning of a post-campaign survey. The ACET telephone questionnaire including some additional campaign related questions took about 7-8 minutes on average to complete. 

As per above, the ACET is based on a survey of the general population and it collects information related to recall of the advertisement; recall of the key messages; who sponsored the ad; determine what, if any action has been taken as a result of seeing the ad; government performance in providing information to the public and overall government performance.

For this evaluation, NRG conducted a general public survey of 2000 Canadians 18 years of age or older.

records.name: 6093c709-2a0d-4c23-867e-27987a79212c
records.notes:
The Skills and Competencies Taxonomy (SCT) streamlines the terminology of hundreds of occupational descriptors used in the labour market. Each descriptor is associated with one of the seven categories (skills, abilities, personal attributes, knowledge, interests, work context and work activities), which are then further divided into sub-categories. 

 

The SCT is one of the two pillars of the Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS), which provides measurement indicators for most descriptors in the Taxonomy across more than 900 occupations. 

 

To improve on the 2019 version, the 2022 SCT was updated with consideration of the content in OaSIS occupational profiles. Among the changes, the category of Personal Abilities and Attributes was split into two distinct categories: Abilities and Personal Attributes. Within the Knowledge category, subcategories and descriptors were redefined to better reflect both academic and technical knowledge and expertise. Finally, various descriptor labels and definitions across the SCT were refined to be more precise, to better capture the intended concepts, and to align with the evolving Canadian labour market.

notes_translated.en:
The Skills and Competencies Taxonomy (SCT) streamlines the terminology of hundreds of occupational descriptors used in the labour market. Each descriptor is associated with one of the seven categories (skills, abilities, personal attributes, knowledge, interests, work context and work activities), which are then further divided into sub-categories. 

 

The SCT is one of the two pillars of the Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS), which provides measurement indicators for most descriptors in the Taxonomy across more than 900 occupations. 

 

To improve on the 2019 version, the 2022 SCT was updated with consideration of the content in OaSIS occupational profiles. Among the changes, the category of Personal Abilities and Attributes was split into two distinct categories: Abilities and Personal Attributes. Within the Knowledge category, subcategories and descriptors were redefined to better reflect both academic and technical knowledge and expertise. Finally, various descriptor labels and definitions across the SCT were refined to be more precise, to better capture the intended concepts, and to align with the evolving Canadian labour market.
notes_translated.fr:
La Taxonomie des compétences et des capacités (TCC) standardise la terminologie de centaines de descripteurs professionnels utilisés sur le marché du travail. Chaque descripteur est associé à l’une des sept catégories (compétences, habiletés, attributs personnels, connaissances, intérêts, contexte de travail et activités de travail), qui sont ensuite subdivisées en sous-catégories. 

 

La TCC constitue l’un des deux piliers du Système d’information sur les professions et les compétences (SIPeC), qui fournit des indicateurs de mesure pour la plupart des descripteurs de la Taxonomie couvrant plus de 900 professions. 

 

Afin d’améliorer la version de 2019, la TCC 2022 a été mise à jour en tenant compte du contenu des profils professionnels du SIPeC. Parmi les changements apportés, la catégorie « Habiletés et attributs personnels » a été scindée en deux catégories distinctes : « Habiletés » et « Attributs personnels ». Dans la catégorie « Connaissances », les sous-catégories et les descripteurs ont été redéfinis afin de mieux refléter les connaissances et l’expertise tant académiques que techniques. Enfin, divers libellés et définitions de descripteurs dans la TCC ont été affinés pour être plus précis, mieux représenter les concepts visés et s’aligner sur l’évolution du marché du travail canadien.

records.name: cfdb703e-b469-43a4-8b96-5debfd09aa3c
records.notes:
The Skills and Competencies Taxonomy (SCT) streamlines the terminology of hundreds of occupational descriptors used in the labour market. Each descriptor is associated with one of the seven categories (skills, abilities, personal attributes, knowledge, interests, work context and work activities), which are then further divided into sub-categories. 

 

The SCT is one of the two pillars of the Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS), which provides measurement indicators for most descriptors in the Taxonomy across more than 900 occupations. 

 

The 2023 version of the SCT introduces updates and improvements to labels and descriptors. Errors were corrected and syntax was refined. Three descriptors were reinstated, and two new descriptors were added under the categories of Abilities and Work Activities.  Additionally, the information derived from the Holland Codes, which forms the basis of the Interests category, was modified to better align the SCT with the OaSIS framework. “Foundational Knowledge” was created under Knowledge category as a new subcategory, and Languages and Mathematics descriptors were moved under this new subcategory.

notes_translated.en:
The Skills and Competencies Taxonomy (SCT) streamlines the terminology of hundreds of occupational descriptors used in the labour market. Each descriptor is associated with one of the seven categories (skills, abilities, personal attributes, knowledge, interests, work context and work activities), which are then further divided into sub-categories. 

 

The SCT is one of the two pillars of the Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS), which provides measurement indicators for most descriptors in the Taxonomy across more than 900 occupations. 

 

The 2023 version of the SCT introduces updates and improvements to labels and descriptors. Errors were corrected and syntax was refined. Three descriptors were reinstated, and two new descriptors were added under the categories of Abilities and Work Activities.  Additionally, the information derived from the Holland Codes, which forms the basis of the Interests category, was modified to better align the SCT with the OaSIS framework. “Foundational Knowledge” was created under Knowledge category as a new subcategory, and Languages and Mathematics descriptors were moved under this new subcategory.
notes_translated.fr:
La Taxonomie des compétences et des capacités (TCC) standardise la terminologie de centaines de descripteurs professionnels utilisés sur le marché du travail. Chaque descripteur est associé à l’une des sept catégories (compétences, habiletés, attributs personnels, connaissances, intérêts, contexte de travail et activités de travail), qui sont ensuite subdivisées en sous-catégories. 

 

La TCC constitue l’un des deux piliers du Système d’information sur les professions et les compétences (SIPeC), qui fournit des indicateurs de mesure pour la plupart des descripteurs de la Taxonomie couvrant plus de 900 professions. 

 

La version 2023 de la TCC introduit des mises à jour et des améliorations aux libellés et aux descripteurs. Les erreurs ont été corrigées et la syntaxe a été affinée. Sous les catégories Habiletés et Activités du travail, trois descripteurs ont été rétablis et deux autres ont été ajoutés. De plus, les informations dérivées des codes Holland, qui constituent la base de la catégorie Intérêts, ont été modifiées afin d’assurer une meilleure harmonisation entre la TCC et le cadre SIPeC. « Connaissances fondamentales » a été créée comme une nouvelle sous‑catégorie dans la catégorie Connaissances, et les descripteurs « Langues » ainsi que « Mathématiques » ont été déplacés sous cette nouvelle sous‑catégorie.

records.name: 10ce43bd-fb58-4969-806b-4bffebc87bec
records.notes:
The OaSIS, OaSIS 2025 Version 1.0, included several updates while maintaining continuity with previous versions. The primary basis for this version of the OaSIS is the **NOC 2021 Version 1.0** and the **SCT 2025 Version 1.0**. However, several important additions and reviews were conducted, which are outlined below.

What’s new:

-	A new feature, **Core competencies**, has been added to highlight key skills, abilities, personal attributes, and knowledge, using a narrative form
-	Three new Abilities descriptors have been rated for all occupations: **General Learning Ability, Hand-Eye Coordination**, and **Form Perception**
-	Ratings for some occupations in the Skills, Abilities, and Knowledge categories were updated
-	The **Skills Match** tool was updated using the revised ratings and the Knowledge category was added
-	The **Skills for Success** section includes data for each profile

This update ensures the database remains accurate and relevant while introducing new features and enhancements.

The OaSIS was published in December 2022 and is based on the **NOC 2021 Version 1.0** and **SCT 2022 Version 1.0**. It includes 900 occupations and information on 200 competency descriptors across the Skills, Abilities, Personal Attributes, Interests, Work Activities and Work Context categories. The profiles also include information from the respective NOC unit groups, including lead statement, example titles, main duties, employment requirements, similar occupations classified elsewhere, workplaces/employers, and additional information.

notes_translated.en:
The OaSIS, OaSIS 2025 Version 1.0, included several updates while maintaining continuity with previous versions. The primary basis for this version of the OaSIS is the **NOC 2021 Version 1.0** and the **SCT 2025 Version 1.0**. However, several important additions and reviews were conducted, which are outlined below.

What’s new:

-	A new feature, **Core competencies**, has been added to highlight key skills, abilities, personal attributes, and knowledge, using a narrative form
-	Three new Abilities descriptors have been rated for all occupations: **General Learning Ability, Hand-Eye Coordination**, and **Form Perception**
-	Ratings for some occupations in the Skills, Abilities, and Knowledge categories were updated
-	The **Skills Match** tool was updated using the revised ratings and the Knowledge category was added
-	The **Skills for Success** section includes data for each profile

This update ensures the database remains accurate and relevant while introducing new features and enhancements.

The OaSIS was published in December 2022 and is based on the **NOC 2021 Version 1.0** and **SCT 2022 Version 1.0**. It includes 900 occupations and information on 200 competency descriptors across the Skills, Abilities, Personal Attributes, Interests, Work Activities and Work Context categories. The profiles also include information from the respective NOC unit groups, including lead statement, example titles, main duties, employment requirements, similar occupations classified elsewhere, workplaces/employers, and additional information.
notes_translated.fr:
Cette édition du SIPeC, soit la version 1.0 du SIPeC de 2025, comprend plusieurs mises à jour, mais préserve la continuité avec les précédentes. Elle se fonde principalement sur la** version 1.0 de la CNP de 2021** et la **version 1.0 de la TCC de 2025**. Plusieurs révisions et ajouts importants ont toutefois été réalisés, lesquels sont présentés ci-dessous.

Nouveauté :

-	Une nouvelle section, à savoir les** Compétences principales**, a été ajouté afin de souligner les compétences, les habiletés, les attributs personnels et les connaissances clés en utilisant une forme narrative.
-	Trois nouveaux descripteurs des Habiletés ont obtenu des cotes pour l’ensemble des professions : l’**Habileté générale à apprendre**, la **Coordination œil-main** et la **Perception des formes**.
-	Des cotes ont été mises à jour dans les catégories Compétences, Habiletés et Connaissances pour certaines professions.
-	L’outil de **Correspondance des compétences** a été mis à jour à l’aide des cotes révisées et la catégorie Connaissances a été ajoutée.
-	La section **Compétences pour réussir** présente maintenant des données pour chaque profil.

Cette mise à jour permet à la base de données de demeurer exacte et pertinente tout en ajoutant des fonctionnalités et des améliorations.

Le  SIPeC a été publiée en décembre 2022 et se fondait sur la **version 1.0 de la CNP de 2021** et la **version 1.0 de la TCC de 2022**. Elle compte 900 professions et des renseignements sur 200 descripteurs de compétences pour les catégories Compétences, Habiletés, Attributs personnels, Domaines d’intérêts, Activités du travail et Contexte de travail. Les profils comprennent aussi de l’information provenant de différents groupes de base de la CNP, dont l’énoncé principal, les exemples d’appellations d’emploi, les fonctions principales, les conditions d’accès à la profession, les occupations similaires classées ailleurs, les milieux de travail et les employeurs et les renseignements supplémentaires.

records.name: 618d2756-8c37-4f99-b184-8b3c1ef1b0f5
records.notes:
The Skills and Competencies Taxonomy (SCT) streamlines the terminology of hundreds of occupational descriptors used in the labour market. Each descriptor is associated with one of the seven categories (skills, abilities, personal attributes, knowledge, interests, work context and work activities), which are then further divided into sub-categories. 

 

The SCT is one of the two pillars of the Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS), which provides measurement indicators for most descriptors in the Taxonomy across more than 900 occupations. 

 

The 2025 version of the SCT was updated to remove all Oxford commas, correct syntax errors, and implement both minor and major changes to labels and descriptor definitions for clarification. Significant changes were made to the Work Activities section as a result of the ongoing development of task statements by profile for the OaSIS. Many labels and descriptor definitions were clarified, and twelve descriptors were removed because they were not mutually exclusive and overlapped with others. The removed descriptors are: Comparing, Document Use, Information Synthesis, Facilitating Group Discussion, Interviewing, Artistic Designing, Catering, Performing Janitorial Services, Constructing, Debugging and Reprogramming, Manipulating– Operating, and Sorting. These descriptors had not been integrated into the OSIS and therefore did not have ratings. In addition, two descriptors were relocated: Working with Computers was moved to Work Context / Structural Job Characteristics, and Writing or Composing was moved from Work Activity / Physically-Oriented Activities to Work Activities / Information-Oriented Activities.  Finally, “Performing administrative activities” was reinstated in the SCT with a modification to its original definition and “Documenting and recording information” reverted to its original definition: Entering, transcribing, recording or maintaining any type of information in written or electronic form. Both descriptors were retained under Work Activities / Information-Oriented Activities.

notes_translated.en:
The Skills and Competencies Taxonomy (SCT) streamlines the terminology of hundreds of occupational descriptors used in the labour market. Each descriptor is associated with one of the seven categories (skills, abilities, personal attributes, knowledge, interests, work context and work activities), which are then further divided into sub-categories. 

 

The SCT is one of the two pillars of the Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS), which provides measurement indicators for most descriptors in the Taxonomy across more than 900 occupations. 

 

The 2025 version of the SCT was updated to remove all Oxford commas, correct syntax errors, and implement both minor and major changes to labels and descriptor definitions for clarification. Significant changes were made to the Work Activities section as a result of the ongoing development of task statements by profile for the OaSIS. Many labels and descriptor definitions were clarified, and twelve descriptors were removed because they were not mutually exclusive and overlapped with others. The removed descriptors are: Comparing, Document Use, Information Synthesis, Facilitating Group Discussion, Interviewing, Artistic Designing, Catering, Performing Janitorial Services, Constructing, Debugging and Reprogramming, Manipulating– Operating, and Sorting. These descriptors had not been integrated into the OSIS and therefore did not have ratings. In addition, two descriptors were relocated: Working with Computers was moved to Work Context / Structural Job Characteristics, and Writing or Composing was moved from Work Activity / Physically-Oriented Activities to Work Activities / Information-Oriented Activities.  Finally, “Performing administrative activities” was reinstated in the SCT with a modification to its original definition and “Documenting and recording information” reverted to its original definition: Entering, transcribing, recording or maintaining any type of information in written or electronic form. Both descriptors were retained under Work Activities / Information-Oriented Activities.
notes_translated.fr:
La Taxonomie des compétences et des capacités (TCC) standardise la terminologie de centaines de descripteurs professionnels utilisés sur le marché du travail. Chaque descripteur est associé à l’une des sept catégories (compétences, habiletés, attributs personnels, connaissances, intérêts, contexte de travail et activités de travail), qui sont ensuite subdivisées en sous-catégories. 

 

La TCC constitue l’un des deux piliers du Système d’information sur les professions et les compétences (SIPeC), qui fournit des indicateurs de mesure pour la plupart des descripteurs de la Taxonomie couvrant plus de 900 professions. 

 

La version 2025 de la TCC a été mise à jour afin de supprimer toutes les virgules dites « Oxford », corriger les erreurs de syntaxe et apporter des modifications mineures et majeures aux libellés et aux définitions des descripteurs, et ce dans un souci de clarification. Des changements importants ont été effectués à la section Activités du travail en raison du développement en cours des énoncés de tâches par profil pour le Système d’information sur les professions et les compétences (SIPeC). Plusieurs libellés et définitions de descripteurs ont été clarifiés, et douze descripteurs ont été supprimés parce qu’ils n’étaient pas mutuellement exclusifs et se chevauchaient avec d’autres. Les descripteurs supprimés sont : Comparaison, Utilisation de documents, Synthèse d’information, Animer des discussions de groupe, Questionner des personnes, Conception artistique, Restauration, Effectuer des services de conciergerie, Construire, Dépanner et reprogrammer, Manipuler – Opérer et Triage. Ces descripteurs n’avaient pas été intégré au SIPeC, ils n’avaient donc pas de cotations. De plus, deux autres descripteurs ont été déplacés : « Travailler avec des ordinateurs » a été déplacé vers Contexte de travail / Caractéristiques structurelles de l’emploi et « Rédaction ou composition » a été déplacé d’Activité du travail / Activités axées sur la dimension physique vers Activités du travail / Activités axées sur l’information. Finalement, « Effectuer des activités administratives » fut rétabli dans la TCC avec une modification de sa définition initiale et « Documenter et enregistrer des renseignements » a repris sa définition initiale :  Saisir, transcrire, consigner, enregistrer ou tenir à jour tout genre d’information sous forme écrite ou électronique. Ces deux descripteurs ont été maintenus sous la catégorie et sous-catégorie : Activités du travail / Activités axées sur l’information.

records.name: eeb3e442-9f19-4d12-8b38-c488fe4f6e5e
records.notes:
The Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) provides a comprehensive framework of the skills and competencies that are usually required to work in different occupations. It also provides context for the work environment in which these occupations are performed.

This dataset provides information on 900 occupations including ratings for over 240 descriptors belonging to one of the following categories: Skills, Abilities, Personal attributes, Work activities and Work context. Standardized dimensions and scales were established at the category level for all categories, except Work context, for which specific dimensions and scales were adopted.

All the descriptors under Skills and Abilities have ratings attributed by proficiency level required by occupation on a scale of 1 to 5. Descriptors under Personal attributes are measured by Importance on a scale of 1 to 5. Descriptors under Work activities are measured by complexity on a scale of 1 to 5. Descriptors under Work context are measured by one or two of the following dimensions: 

- **Frequency (1-5 scale)**
    - 1 - Once a year or more but not every month 
    - 2 - Once a month or more but not every week
    - 3 - Once a week or more but not every day
    - 4 - Every day, a few times per day
    - 5 - Every day, many times per day
- **Duration (1-5 scale)**
    - 1 - Very little time
    - 2 - Less than half the time
    - 3 - About half the time
    - 4 - More than half the time
    - 5 - All the time, or almost all the time
- Degree of responsibility (1-5 scale)
- Physical distance from others (1-5 scale)
- Degree of consequence of error (1-5 scale)
- Degree of impact (1-5 scale)
- Degree of freedom to make decisions (1-5 scale)
- Degree of freedom to determine tasks and priorities (1-5 scale)
- Degree of competition (1-5 scale)
- Degree of automation (1-3 scale)
- Work schedule (1-3 scale)
- Worked hours in a typical week (1-3 scale)

This dataset is of national relevance.

notes_translated.en:

notes_translated.fr:
Le Système d’information sur les professions et les compétences (SIPeC) fournit un cadre complet des compétences et des capacités habituellement requises pour travailler dans différentes professions. Il fournit également un contexte pour l'environnement de travail dans lequel ces professions sont exercées.

Cet ensemble de données fournit des informations sur 900 professions, y compris des cotes pour plus de 240 descripteurs appartenant à l'une des catégories suivantes : Compétences, Habiletés, Attributs personnels, Activités du travail et Contexte de travail. Des dimensions et échelles standardisées ont été établies au niveau des catégories pour toutes les catégories, à l'exception du Contexte de travail, pour lequel des dimensions et échelles spécifiques ont été adoptées.

Tous les descripteurs sous Compétences et Habiletés ont des cotes attribuées selon le niveau de compétence requis par la profession sur une échelle de 1 à 5. Les descripteurs sous Attributs personnels sont mesurés par importance sur une échelle de 1 à 5. Les descripteurs sous Activités de travail sont mesurés par complexité sur une échelle de 1 à 5. Les descripteurs sous Contexte de travail sont mesurés par une ou deux des dimensions suivantes :

- **Fréquence (Échelle : 1-5)**
    - 1 - Une fois ou plus par année mais pas à chaque mois
    - 2 - Une fois ou plus par mois mais pas à chaque semaine
    - 3 - Une fois ou plus par semaine mais pas à chaque jour
    - 4 - Tous les jours, quelques fois par jour
    - 5 - Tous les jours, plusieurs fois par jour
- **Durée (Échelle : 1-5)**
    - 1 - Très peu de temps
    - 2 - Moins de la moitié du temps
    - 3 - Environ la moitié du temps
    - 4 - Plus de la moitié du temps
    - 5 - Tout le temps, ou presque tout le temps
- Degré de responsabilité (Échelle : 1-5)
- La distance physique des autres (Échelle : 1-5)
- Degré de conséquence de l’erreur (Échelle : 1-5)
- Degré d’impact (Échelle : 1-5)
- Degré de liberté de décision (Échelle : 1-5)
- Degré de liberté pour déterminer les taches et les priorités (Échelle : 1-5)
- Degré de compétition (Échelle : 1-5)
- Degré d’automatisation (Échelle : 1-3)
- Horaire de travail (Échelle : 1-3)
- Heures travaillées dans une semaine typique (Échelle : 1-3)

Ce jeu de données est représentatif au niveau national.

The Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) provides a comprehensive framework of the skills and competencies that are usually required to work in different occupations. It also provides context for the work environment in which these occupations are performed.

This dataset provides information on 900 occupations including ratings for over 240 descriptors belonging to one of the following categories: Skills, Abilities, Personal attributes, Knowledge, Interests, Work activities and Work context. Descriptors under Interests are measured by predominance ranking of Holland Codes. Descriptors under Work activities are measured by complexity on a scale of 1 to 5. records.name: 4fa81aa5-20ac-49a0-aee1-b3a398abc1af
records.notes: CSC remains committed to developing and maintaining a strong, inclusive, and diverse workforce that not only reflects the Canadian population, but also the populations we serve. We don’t just accept diversity – we celebrate it! We are making targeted efforts to recruit and retain diverse employees. In fact, CSC is the largest employer of Indigenous peoples in the core public administration. We believe that our ability to deliver accessible and inclusive services is realized when we recognize and retain the most diverse range of thoughts, experiences, and skills.

notes_translated.fr: Le SCC demeure déterminé à développer et à maintenir un effectif solide, inclusif et diversifié qui reflète non seulement la population canadienne, mais aussi les populations que nous servons. Nous n'acceptons pas seulement la diversité, nous la célébrons! Nous déployons des efforts ciblés pour recruter et retenir des employé(e)s diversifié(e)s. En fait, le SCC est le plus grand employeur de personnes Autochtones au sein de l'administration publique centrale. Nous croyons que notre capacité à fournir des services accessibles et inclusifs se réalise lorsque nous reconnaissons et conservons l'éventail le plus diversifié de pensées, d'expériences et de compétences.
notes_translated.en: CSC remains committed to developing and maintaining a strong, inclusive, and diverse workforce that not only reflects the Canadian population, but also the populations we serve. We don’t just accept diversity – we celebrate it! We are making targeted efforts to recruit and retain diverse employees. In fact, CSC is the largest employer of Indigenous peoples in the core public administration. We believe that our ability to deliver accessible and inclusive services is realized when we recognize and retain the most diverse range of thoughts, experiences, and skills.

records.name: 51ed1d2d-7f9a-4ebe-a281-5439066e8524
records.notes: Public school enrolments in regular programs for youth in elementary and secondary schools, by Indigenous identity, age and sex. Counts represent the number of students enrolled in off-reserve public schools and self-identified as belonging to one of three Indigenous groups recognized by the Canadian Constitution: First Nations (North American Indian), Métis and Inuit (Inuk).

notes_translated.en: Public school enrolments in regular programs for youth in elementary and secondary schools, by Indigenous identity, age and sex. Counts represent the number of students enrolled in off-reserve public schools and self-identified as belonging to one of three Indigenous groups recognized by the Canadian Constitution: First Nations (North American Indian), Métis and Inuit (Inuk).
notes_translated.fr: Effectif scolaire des programmes réguliers pour les jeunes dans les écoles primaires et secondaires publiques, selon l’identité autochtone, l'âge et le sexe. L’effectif représente le nombre d’élèves inscrits à une école publique hors réserve et qui ont déclaré appartenir à l’un des trois groupes autochtones reconnus par la Constitution canadienne, soit les Premières Nations (Indiens de l’Amérique du Nord), les Métis et les Inuits.

records.name: 404fbfa7-6954-489b-bf92-9a5efa36dbf4
records.notes: [Supporting Ontario’s Safe Employers](https://www.ontario.ca/page/supporting-ontarios-safe-employers-program) is a voluntary program that promotes health and safety in the workplace and helps reduce injuries and illness. The Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development runs the program under the direction of the Chief Prevention Officer (CPO). Employers that are recognized by the program have successfully implemented an occupational health and safety management system and [met other criteria set by the CPO](https://www.ontario.ca/page/chief-prevention-officer-employer-recognition-criteria).

notes_translated.en: [Supporting Ontario’s Safe Employers](https://www.ontario.ca/page/supporting-ontarios-safe-employers-program) is a voluntary program that promotes health and safety in the workplace and helps reduce injuries and illness. The Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development runs the program under the direction of the Chief Prevention Officer (CPO). Employers that are recognized by the program have successfully implemented an occupational health and safety management system and [met other criteria set by the CPO](https://www.ontario.ca/page/chief-prevention-officer-employer-recognition-criteria).
notes_translated.fr: [Reconnaissance des employeurs pour la sécurité au travail en Ontario](https://www.ontario.ca/fr/page/reconnaissance-des-employeurs-pour-la-securite-au-travail-en-ontario) est un programme volontaire qui promeut la santé et la sécurité sur le lieu de travail et contribue à réduire les blessures et les maladies. Le ministère du Travail, de l’Immigration, de la Formation et du Développement des compétences gère le programme sous la direction du directeur général de la prévention (DGP). Les employeurs reconnus par le programme ont mis en œuvre avec succès un système de gestion de la santé et de la sécurité au travail et ont [satisfait à d’autres critères établis par le DGP](https://www.ontario.ca/fr/page/reconnaissance-des-employeurs-pour-la-securite-au-travail-en-ontario).

records.name: a3f75316-2ee3-40f3-ab22-a0611a450952
records.notes: Inventory of employer and union relationships in Ontario including employer and union names, collective agreement start and expiry dates, number of employees, labour legislation, industry and sector. This data is available on the Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development website under the [Labour Relations Collective Agreements e-Library Portal](https://www.lr.labour.gov.on.ca/en-CA/Collective-Agreements/). [Section 90](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/95l01#BK106) of Labour Relations Act requires all employers and unions to file a copy of their collective agreements with the Minister, and the dataset includes, where available, only a listing of the copies of collective agreements filed, and does not purport to be comprehensive. The Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development is not liable for any amendments, errors and omissions in the dataset as the information is gathered as made available after filed with the Minister.

notes_translated.en: Inventory of employer and union relationships in Ontario including employer and union names, collective agreement start and expiry dates, number of employees, labour legislation, industry and sector. This data is available on the Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development website under the [Labour Relations Collective Agreements e-Library Portal](https://www.lr.labour.gov.on.ca/en-CA/Collective-Agreements/). [Section 90](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/95l01#BK106) of Labour Relations Act requires all employers and unions to file a copy of their collective agreements with the Minister, and the dataset includes, where available, only a listing of the copies of collective agreements filed, and does not purport to be comprehensive. The Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development is not liable for any amendments, errors and omissions in the dataset as the information is gathered as made available after filed with the Minister.
notes_translated.fr: Inventaire des relations patronales et syndicales en Ontario, y compris l'employeur et les noms syndicaux, debut de l'entente collective et les dates d'expiration, le nombre d'employes, la legislation du travail, l'industrie et le secteur. Disponible sur le site Web du Ministère du Travail, de l’Immigration, de la Formation et du Développement des compétences par le biais du [portail Bibliotheque electronique des conventions collectives](https://www.lr.labour.gov.on.ca/fr-CA/Collective-Agreements/). [L'article 90](https://www.ontario.ca/fr/lois/loi/95l01) de la Loi sur les relations de travail exige que tous les employeurs et les syndicats deposent une copie de leurs conventions collectives aupres du Ministere, et l'ensemble de donnees comprend, le cas echeant, seulement une liste des copies des conventions collectives deposees, et ne pretend pas etre exhaustif. Le Ministère du Travail, de l’Immigration, de la Formation et du Développement n'est pas tenu responsable de toute modification, erreur et omission dans l'ensemble de donnees, etant donne que l'information est recueillie a partir des donnees deposees aupres du ministere.

records.name: 8d8c31d3-4ec8-4684-b817-39ce2884bb2f
records.notes: A claim is a complaint made by a person who believes their employer has violated the Employment Standards Act, 2000. Where an Officer finds a violation of the Act, they assess money owing. This data is regularly updated and is subject to change. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) information at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources.

notes_translated.en: A claim is a complaint made by a person who believes their employer has violated the Employment Standards Act, 2000. Where an Officer finds a violation of the Act, they assess money owing. This data is regularly updated and is subject to change. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) information at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources.
notes_translated.fr: Une reclamation est une plainte deposee par une personne qui estime que son employeur a viole la Loi de 2000 sur les normes d'emploi. Si l'agent conclut qu'il y a eu une violation de la loi, il evalue les montants dus a la personne. Ces donnees sont regulierement mises a jour et peuvent donc changer. Les donnees provenant du ministere du Travail produisent des donnees sur la sante et la securite au travail et sur les normes d'emploi, a une date donnee et a des fins de rapport precis. En consequence, les renseignements susmentionnes pourraient etre differents de ceux qui emanent d'autres sources de donnees.

records.name: 02d55a16-541c-4f3d-a305-31b624015329
records.notes: This Alberta Official Statistic describes the employment rates of persons aged 15 years and over by highest level of education for Alberta and Canada from 1990 to 2014. The educational classifications are '0-8 years', ‘some high school’, 'high school graduate', ‘some post-secondary’, 'post-secondary certificate or diploma', ‘bachelor’s degree’ and 'above bachelor’s degree'. Generally, persons with higher educational qualifications have higher employment rates than those with lower qualifications.

notes_translated.en: This Alberta Official Statistic describes the employment rates of persons aged 15 years and over by highest level of education for Alberta and Canada from 1990 to 2014. The educational classifications are '0-8 years', ‘some high school’, 'high school graduate', ‘some post-secondary’, 'post-secondary certificate or diploma', ‘bachelor’s degree’ and 'above bachelor’s degree'. Generally, persons with higher educational qualifications have higher employment rates than those with lower qualifications.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: Cette statistique officielle de l'Alberta décrit les taux d'emploi des personnes âgées de 15 ans et plus selon le plus haut niveau de scolarité en Alberta et au Canada de 1990 à 2014. Les classifications scolaires sont les suivantes : « 0 à 8 ans », « certains diplômés du secondaire », « certains diplômés du secondaire », « certificat ou diplôme d'études postsecondaires », « baccalauréat » et « diplôme supérieur au baccalauréat ». En général, les personnes ayant un niveau d'études plus élevé ont des taux d'emploi plus élevés que les personnes moins qualifiées.

records.name: 1d0ff3cc-c741-4151-ad75-c306951ecf0a
records.notes: This Alberta Official Statistic describes employment rates for off-reserve Aboriginal people and non-Aboriginal people living in Alberta for the period 2003-2014. The Employment Rate is a measure of the proportion of the working age population (15 years of age and over) who are employed. The Labour Force Survey provides estimates of employment and unemployment which are among the most timely and important measures of performance of the Canadian economy. In 2004, a question was added in the LFS for the western provinces to differentiate off-reserve Aboriginal people in the working age population.

notes_translated.en: This Alberta Official Statistic describes employment rates for off-reserve Aboriginal people and non-Aboriginal people living in Alberta for the period 2003-2014. The Employment Rate is a measure of the proportion of the working age population (15 years of age and over) who are employed. The Labour Force Survey provides estimates of employment and unemployment which are among the most timely and important measures of performance of the Canadian economy. In 2004, a question was added in the LFS for the western provinces to differentiate off-reserve Aboriginal people in the working age population.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: Cette statistique officielle de l'Alberta décrit les taux d'emploi des Autochtones vivant hors réserve et des non-Autochtones vivant en Alberta pour la période 2003-2014. Le taux d'emploi est une mesure de la proportion de la population en âge de travailler (15 ans et plus) qui a un emploi. L'Enquête sur la population active fournit des estimations de l'emploi et du chômage qui figurent parmi les mesures les plus récentes et les plus importantes de la performance de l'économie canadienne. En 2004, une question a été ajoutée à la LFS pour les provinces de l'Ouest afin de différencier les Autochtones vivant hors réserve de la population en âge de travailler.

records.notes: This dataset contains financial data for service delivery sites contracted to deliver the Employment Service (ES) program for the 2022-23 fiscal year. The financial data includes the amount of funding allocated and the Ministry approved expenditure amount. Service delivery sites are operated by service providers who hold the funding agreement with the Ministry, and one service provider can operate multiple service delivery sites. The values are broken down by the different budget lines for ES.

notes_translated.en: This dataset contains financial data for service delivery sites contracted to deliver the Employment Service (ES) program for the 2022-23 fiscal year. The financial data includes the amount of funding allocated and the Ministry approved expenditure amount. Service delivery sites are operated by service providers who hold the funding agreement with the Ministry, and one service provider can operate multiple service delivery sites. The values are broken down by the different budget lines for ES.
notes_translated.fr: Cet ensemble de données contient des données financières se rapportant aux points de service sous contrat avec le programme des Services d’emploi (SE) pour l’exercice 2022 2023. Les données financières comprennent le montant du financement alloué et le montant des dépenses approuvées par le ministère. Les points de service sont gérés par des fournisseurs de services qui détiennent une entente de financement conclue avec le ministère, et un fournisseur de services peut gérer plusieurs points de service. Les valeurs sont ventilées selon les différents postes budgétaires des SE.

records.name: 61c2828a-90aa-4b78-8183-6220615e3219
records.notes: *Please note, as of April 3, 2019, collection and reporting of this data has been discontinued by the ministry. There will be no further updates to this dataset.* Get aggregate data on total employer applications for Employment Standards Act Hours of Work (HOW) approvals. An Hours of Work (HOW) application is a request made by an employer with agreement from the employee (or employees) or union to permit employees to work excess hours over the established maximum (as per the [Employment Standards Act (2000) section 17](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/00e41?_ga=2.227873885.1938154121.1583253197-539827471.1558961448#BK32)) or to average their overtime hours over two or more weeks for purposes of calculating overtime pay (as per the [Employment Standards Act (2000) section 22](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/00e41?_ga=2.227873885.1938154121.1583253197-539827471.1558961448#BK41).

notes_translated.en: *Please note, as of April 3, 2019, collection and reporting of this data has been discontinued by the ministry. There will be no further updates to this dataset.* Get aggregate data on total employer applications for Employment Standards Act Hours of Work (HOW) approvals. An Hours of Work (HOW) application is a request made by an employer with agreement from the employee (or employees) or union to permit employees to work excess hours over the established maximum (as per the [Employment Standards Act (2000) section 17](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/00e41?_ga=2.227873885.1938154121.1583253197-539827471.1558961448#BK32)) or to average their overtime hours over two or more weeks for purposes of calculating overtime pay (as per the [Employment Standards Act (2000) section 22](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/00e41?_ga=2.227873885.1938154121.1583253197-539827471.1558961448#BK41).
notes_translated.fr: *Les données ne sont plus collectées et divulguées par le ministère depuis le 3 avril 2019. Il n’y aura pas d’autres mises à jour de cet ensemble de données.* btenez des données agrégées sur le nombre total de demandes d’approbation des heures de travail présentées par les employeurs en vertu de la Loi sur les normes d’emploi. Une demande relative aux heures de travail est une demande présentée par un employeur et qui est autorisée par un employé ou des employés ou un syndicat, afin de permettre à un ou des employés d’effectuer des heures dont le nombre est supérieur au nombre maximal autorisé (conformément à [l’article 17 de la Loi de 2000 sur les normes d’emploi](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/00e41?_ga=2.227873885.1938154121.1583253197-539827471.1558961448#BK32) , ou de permettre le calcul de la moyenne des heures supplémentaires sur une période de deux semaines ou plus aux fins du calcul des heures supplémentaires (conformément [à l’article 22 de la Loi de 2000 sur les normes d’emploi](https://www.ontario.ca/laws/statute/00e41?_ga=2.227873885.1938154121.1583253197-539827471.1558961448#BK41))

records.notes: This Alberta Official Statistic describes the educational attainment of employed off-reserve Aboriginal and non-Aboriginal Albertans aged 15 years and over. More specifically, the population is split into 4 subgroups (All Aboriginals, First Nations, Métis and Non-Aboriginal) and the educational attainment is also split into 4 subgroups on the chart (University Degree, Post-secondary certificate or diploma, High School graduate or some post-secondary, and Less than High School).

notes_translated.en: This Alberta Official Statistic describes the educational attainment of employed off-reserve Aboriginal and non-Aboriginal Albertans aged 15 years and over. More specifically, the population is split into 4 subgroups (All Aboriginals, First Nations, Métis and Non-Aboriginal) and the educational attainment is also split into 4 subgroups on the chart (University Degree, Post-secondary certificate or diploma, High School graduate or some post-secondary, and Less than High School).
notes_translated.fr-t-en: Cette statistique officielle de l'Alberta décrit le niveau de scolarité des Albertains autochtones et non autochtones employés hors réserve âgés de 15 ans et plus. Plus précisément, la population est divisée en 4 sous-groupes (tous les Autochtones, les Premières Nations, les Métis et les non-Autochtones) et le niveau de scolarité est également divisé en 4 sous-groupes sur le graphique (diplôme universitaire, certificat ou diplôme d'études postsecondaires, diplôme d'études secondaires ou partiellement postsecondaires, et études secondaires inférieures).

notes_translated.en: The Ontario Collective Agreements Interactive Search provides a searchable listing of collective agreements in Ontario. The information in this report is compiled from collective agreement settlements reported to Labour Relations Information Bureau (LRIB). The data reflects settlements for Ontario-based employees covered under Ontario or Federal jurisdictions.
notes_translated.fr: La recherche interactive des conventions collectives de l'Ontario fournit une liste consultable des conventions collectives de l'Ontario. Les renseignements contenus dans ce rapport sont compilés à partir des accords de convention collectives signalés au Bureau d'information sur les relations de travail (BIRT). Les données transmettent les accords applicables aux employés gérés en Ontario et couverts par les compétences ontariennes ou fédérales.

records.notes: This Alberta Official Statistic compares the unemployment rates of Canada and the ten provinces from 1976 to 2014. The unemployment rate is a measure of the proportion of people in the labour force who are unemployed. The labour force includes individuals 15 years and over who are employed or unemployed and looking for work.

notes_translated.en: This Alberta Official Statistic compares the unemployment rates of Canada and the ten provinces from 1976 to 2014. The unemployment rate is a measure of the proportion of people in the labour force who are unemployed. The labour force includes individuals 15 years and over who are employed or unemployed and looking for work.
notes_translated.fr-t-en: Cette statistique officielle de l'Alberta compare les taux de chômage du Canada et des dix provinces de 1976 à 2014. Le taux de chômage est une mesure de la proportion de personnes au chômage dans la population active. La population active comprend les personnes âgées de 15 ans et plus qui ont un emploi ou sont au chômage et qui recherchent un emploi.

records.name: 8af112fb-6125-4172-ba58-d00e2ec09ae3
records.notes: The Collective Agreement e-Library is a self-serve, online portal that houses public and private sector collective agreements in Ontario. The Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development is making it easier for Ontarians to find the information they want, when they want, as part of Ontario's Open Government commitment. Section 90 of the Labour Relations Act, 1995, requires that parties to collective agreements file those agreements with the Minister. In order to comply with that requirement, parties can submit their collective agreements [online](https://www.lrs.labour.gov.on.ca/en/file-collective-agreement.htm).

notes_translated.en: The Collective Agreement e-Library is a self-serve, online portal that houses public and private sector collective agreements in Ontario. The Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development is making it easier for Ontarians to find the information they want, when they want, as part of Ontario's Open Government commitment. Section 90 of the Labour Relations Act, 1995, requires that parties to collective agreements file those agreements with the Minister. In order to comply with that requirement, parties can submit their collective agreements [online](https://www.lrs.labour.gov.on.ca/en/file-collective-agreement.htm).
notes_translated.fr: Le portail de la bibliothèque électronique des conventions collectives est un libre-service en ligne qui contient les conventions collectives du secteur privé et public de l'Ontario. Le Ministère du Travail, de l’Immigration, de la Formation et du Développement des compétences facilite la tâche aux Ontariennes et Ontariens en vue de leur permettre de trouver les informations qu'ils recherchent quand ils les recherchent, conformément à l'engagement du gouvernement ontarien concernant l'initiative pour un gouvernement ouvert. Article 90 de la Loi de 1995 sur les relations de travail exige que les parties aux conventions collectives en dépose une copie auprès du ministre. Pour être en conformité avec cette obligation, les parties peuvent soumettre leurs conventions collectives [en ligne](https://www.lrs.labour.gov.on.ca/fr/file-collective-agreement.htm).

records.notes: When an inspector finds that health and safety legislation or regulations are not being complied with, a written order is issued to comply with the legislation/regulation within a certain time period. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) are captured at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources.

notes_translated.en: When an inspector finds that health and safety legislation or regulations are not being complied with, a written order is issued to comply with the legislation/regulation within a certain time period. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) are captured at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources.
notes_translated.fr: Lorsqu'un inspecteur estime qu'une loi ou un reglement en matiere de sante et securite au travail n'est pas suivi, il rend un ordre ecrit exigeant que l'employeur se conforme a la loi ou au reglement vise dans un delai precis. Les donnees provenant du ministere du Travail produisent des donnees sur la sante et la securite au travail et sur les normes d'emploi, a une date donnee et a des fins de rapport precis. En consequence, les renseignements susmentionnes pourraient etre differents de ceux qui emanent d'autres sources de donnees.

records.notes: The Ministry of Labour inspects workplaces to monitor compliance with occupational health and safety legislation and regulations. These are considered to be proactive field visits. The ministry focuses on workplaces and/or sectors of the economy that have a history of poor compliance or high levels of work-related injuries. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) information at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources.

notes_translated.en: The Ministry of Labour inspects workplaces to monitor compliance with occupational health and safety legislation and regulations. These are considered to be proactive field visits. The ministry focuses on workplaces and/or sectors of the economy that have a history of poor compliance or high levels of work-related injuries. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) information at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources.
notes_translated.fr: Le ministere du Travail inspecte des lieux de travail afin de surveiller la conformite aux lois et reglements en matiere de sante et securite au travail. Ces inspections sont des visites sur place proactives. Le ministere met l'accent sur les lieux de travail et les secteurs de l'economie qui ont des antecedents de faible conformite aux lois et reglements et des niveaux eleves de blessures au travail. Les donnees provenant du ministere du Travail produisent des donnees sur la sante et la securite au travail et sur les normes d'emploi, a une date donnee et a des fins de rapport precis. En consequence, les renseignements susmentionnes pourraient etre differents de ceux qui emanent d'autres sources de donnees.

records.name: 1e702c06-9711-451e-acc2-e79c18d205d7
records.notes: This dataset provides total numbers of events that have been reported to the Ministry of Labour. Events are categorized as work refusals, complaints, incidents, illnesses, occurrences, disputes or work stoppages registered with the ministry regarding health and safety issues. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) information at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources From 2011/2012 to 2014/2015, the date range collected is based on the 'event date' data field from the Ministry of Labour Case Management System. This data is a total number of unique ministry event IDs. Data are subject to change due to updates to the case management system. *[IDs]: identifications

notes_translated.en: This dataset provides total numbers of events that have been reported to the Ministry of Labour. Events are categorized as work refusals, complaints, incidents, illnesses, occurrences, disputes or work stoppages registered with the ministry regarding health and safety issues. Data from the Ministry of Labour reflects Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) and Employment Standards (ES) information at a point in time and/or for specific reporting purposes. As a result, the information above may not align with other data sources From 2011/2012 to 2014/2015, the date range collected is based on the 'event date' data field from the Ministry of Labour Case Management System. This data is a total number of unique ministry event IDs. Data are subject to change due to updates to the case management system. *[IDs]: identifications
notes_translated.fr: Cet ensemble de donnees contient le nombre total d'incidents qui ont ete signales au ministere du Travail. Les incidents sont classes en plusieurs categories : refus de travailler, plaintes, incidents, maladies, occurences, differends ou arrets des travaux inscrits aupres du ministere pour des raisons de sante et securite. Les donnees provenant du ministere du Travail produisent des donnees sur la sante et la securite au travail et sur les normes d'emploi, a une date donnee et a des fins de rapport precis. En consequence, les renseignements susmentionnes pourraient etre differents de ceux qui emanent d'autres sources de donnees. De 2011-2012 a 2014-2015, l'eventail des donnees se fonde sur la date de l'incident telle qu'elle apparait dans le systeme de gestion des cas du ministere du Travail. Cette donnee represente le nombre total de numeros d'identite d'incidents du ministere. Les donnees peuvent etre changees chaque fois que le systeme de gestion des cas est mis a jour.

records.name: 01742c1e-4d99-485c-a2a4-c51163da542f
records.notes: A field visit occurs when a ministry inspector visits a workplace to assess compliance with the Occupational Health and Safety Act (OHSA) and regulations. A workplace is considered any land, premises, physical location or thing at, upon, in or near which a worker work.  An inspector may visit the same workplace multiple times. During the field visit, the inspector can issue orders to different companies ("contraveners") at a workplace to achieve compliance with OHSA and regulations. Inspectors can also issue legal direction to workplace parties notifying them of their obligation to co-operate and to provide requested information. This dataset includes the: * date of the field visit * type of visit (for example, initial or follow-up) * case type (inspection or investigation) * case status * workplace visited, including workplace ID, name, location and NAICS code * contravener's name and role, where applicable * orders issued under OHSA and regulations, where applicable *[OHSA]: Occupational Health and Safety Act *[NAICS]: North American Industry Classification System

notes_translated.en: A field visit occurs when a ministry inspector visits a workplace to assess compliance with the Occupational Health and Safety Act (OHSA) and regulations. A workplace is considered any land, premises, physical location or thing at, upon, in or near which a worker work.  An inspector may visit the same workplace multiple times. During the field visit, the inspector can issue orders to different companies ("contraveners") at a workplace to achieve compliance with OHSA and regulations. Inspectors can also issue legal direction to workplace parties notifying them of their obligation to co-operate and to provide requested information. This dataset includes the: * date of the field visit * type of visit (for example, initial or follow-up) * case type (inspection or investigation) * case status * workplace visited, including workplace ID, name, location and NAICS code * contravener's name and role, where applicable * orders issued under OHSA and regulations, where applicable *[OHSA]: Occupational Health and Safety Act *[NAICS]: North American Industry Classification System
notes_translated.fr: Une visite d'inspection a lieu lorsqu'un inspecteur du ministere se rend a un lieu de travail pour evaluer la conformite a la _Loi sur la sant e et la securite au travail_ (LSST) et ses reglements. Un lieu de travail est un bien- fonds, un local, un lieu ou un objet sur lequel ou pres duquel un travailleur travaille. Un inspecteur peut visiter le meme lieu de travail plusieurs fois. Au cours de la visite sur place, l'inspecteur peut emettre des ordres a differentes societes (les « contrevenants ») dans un lieu de travail afin d'assurer la conformite a la LSST et a ses reglements. Les inspecteurs peuvent aussi rendre des directives juridiques aux personnes presentes dans le lieu de travail pour les aviser de leur obligation de collaborer et de fournir les informations demandees. Cet ensemble de donnees comprend : * la date de la visite; * le type de visite (par exemple, initiale, suivi); * le type de cas (inspection ou enquete); * l'etat du cas; * le lieu de travail visite, dont le no d'identification, le nom, le lieu et le code SCIAN du lieu de travail); * le nom et le role du contrevenant, le cas echeant; * les ordres rendus en vertu de la LSST et de ses reglements, le cas echeant. *[LSST]: Loi sur la santé et la sécurité au travail *[SCIAN]: Système de classification des industries de l'Amérique du Nord

data_series_name.en: Canada Summer Jobs: Organizations that received funding and the number of jobs they created
data_series_name.fr: Emplois d’été Canada : Organisations qui ont reçu du financement et le nombre d'emplois créés

records.name: 36f33d8b-8527-48c5-adff-343639ec4b50
records.notes:
This dataset contains a list of organizations funded under the Canada Summer Jobs programs during the years of 2017 to 2025.

Canada Summer Jobs (CSJ) is a national program delivered solely by ESDC. CSJ supports employers from not-for-profit organizations and the public sector, as well as private sector organizations with 50 or fewer full-time employees, to provide quality summer employment opportunities for youth (age 15 to 30). CSJ provides youth with opportunities to develop and improve their skills.  For some, CSJ is a first job experience that informs future education, training, and career choices. The program is responsive to national and local priorities, as well as labour market needs.

notes_translated.en:
This dataset contains a list of organizations funded under the Canada Summer Jobs programs during the years of 2017 to 2025.

Canada Summer Jobs (CSJ) is a national program delivered solely by ESDC. CSJ supports employers from not-for-profit organizations and the public sector, as well as private sector organizations with 50 or fewer full-time employees, to provide quality summer employment opportunities for youth (age 15 to 30). CSJ provides youth with opportunities to develop and improve their skills.  For some, CSJ is a first job experience that informs future education, training, and career choices. The program is responsive to national and local priorities, as well as labour market needs.
notes_translated.fr:
Cet ensemble de données contient une liste d'organisations financées dans le cadre du programme Emplois d'été Canada au cours des années 2017 à 2025.

Emplois d’été Canada (EEC) est un programme national mis en œuvre uniquement par EDSC. Il soutient les organismes à but non lucratif, les employeurs du secteur public, ainsi que les employeurs du secteur privé comptant 50 employés ou moins travaillant à temps plein, afin qu’ils créent des occasions d’emploi d’été de qualité pour les jeunes (âgés de 15 à 30 ans). EEC offre aux jeunes la possibilité de perfectionner leurs compétences et d’en acquérir de nouvelles. Certains pourront vivre leur première expérience professionnelle dans le cadre du programme, ce qui leur permettra d’orienter les choix qu’ils feront dans l’avenir en matière d’éducation, de formation et de carrière. Le programme répond aux priorités nationales et locales, ainsi qu’aux besoins du marché du travail.

records.name: d5037685-6fc2-46df-abfe-5486bd62dbcf
records.notes:
Vocational Rehabilitation Services is a component of the Veterans Affairs Rehabilitation Program. It provides vocational assistance services and benefits to eligible Veterans who have a health problem, and their families, to assist them in their re-establishment to civilian life. Vocational services and benefits include vocational assessments, employability skills, training, career exploration, job placement and follow-up support. 

The purpose of the audit was to provide assurance that financial controls were working as intended, to deliver results in accordance with related authorities. This is the first audit of Vocational Rehabilitation since its inception in 2006.

notes_translated.en:
Vocational Rehabilitation Services is a component of the Veterans Affairs Rehabilitation Program. It provides vocational assistance services and benefits to eligible Veterans who have a health problem, and their families, to assist them in their re-establishment to civilian life. Vocational services and benefits include vocational assessments, employability skills, training, career exploration, job placement and follow-up support. 

The purpose of the audit was to provide assurance that financial controls were working as intended, to deliver results in accordance with related authorities. This is the first audit of Vocational Rehabilitation since its inception in 2006.
notes_translated.fr:
Les services de réadaptation professionnelle sont un volet du Programme de réadaptation d’Anciens Combattants Canada. Ce volet offre des services de réadaptation professionnelle et des avantages connexes aux vétérans admissibles qui ont un problème de santé, ainsi qu’aux membres de leur famille, pour les aider à réintégrer la vie civile. Parmi ces services et ces avantages, mentionnons les évaluations professionnelles, l’apprentissage de compétences améliorant l’employabilité, la formation, la prospection de carrières, l’aide au placement et le soutien de suivi. 

L’objectif de la vérification était de s’assurer que les contrôles financiers fonctionnent comme prévu et qu’ils donnent les résultats escomptés, conformément aux autorisations connexes. Il s’agit de la première vérification du volet Réadaptation professionnelle depuis sa création en 2006.

records.name: eaaf7c87-8ffe-4d68-8110-91b533da2b6f
records.notes:
This edition of the Career Handbook includes 939 occupational profiles that provide updated information in the following sections: Lead Statement, Examples of Job Titles, Workplaces/Employers, Employment Requirements, Exclusions, Occupational Options and Remarks. No updates have been made in the Career Handbook-specific content. This means that the profiles maintain the original descriptor scales and ratings for aptitudes, interests, involvement with data/people/things, physical activities and environmental conditions, as developed in the CH 2003.

This counselling resource is used by a wide range of professionals for many applications, and by individuals engaged in self-directed career planning.

notes_translated.fr:
Cette édition du Guide sur les carrières comprend 939 profils professionnels qui fournissent de l’information mise à jour dans les sections suivantes : Énoncé principal, Exemples d’appellations d’emploi, Milieu de travail/employeur, Conditions d’accès à la profession, Exclusions, Options professionnelles et Notes. Aucune mise à jour n'a été effectuée dans le contenu spécifique au Guide sur les carrières. Cela signifie que les profils maintiennent les échelles et les cotes originales pour les aptitudes, les intérêts, le travail avec données/personnes/choses, les activités physiques et les conditions environnementales, tel que développées dans le Guide sur les carrières 2003.

Cette ressource d'orientation est utilisée par un large éventail de professionnels pour de nombreuses applications, ainsi que par des individus engagés dans une planification de carrière autodirigée.
notes_translated.en:
This edition of the Career Handbook includes 939 occupational profiles that provide updated information in the following sections: Lead Statement, Examples of Job Titles, Workplaces/Employers, Employment Requirements, Exclusions, Occupational Options and Remarks. No updates have been made in the Career Handbook-specific content. This means that the profiles maintain the original descriptor scales and ratings for aptitudes, interests, involvement with data/people/things, physical activities and environmental conditions, as developed in the CH 2003.

This counselling resource is used by a wide range of professionals for many applications, and by individuals engaged in self-directed career planning.
